title: create.sh public output is already level-only
summary: Live create.sh help, normal stdout, JSON stdout, phase logs, and sharded warnings use --level, DOC_LEVEL, Level N, phase, and sharded vocabulary without exposing preset/capability/kind workflow terms.
evidence: .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/create.sh --help prints `Creates a new spec folder with templates based on documentation level.`
Normal stdout prints `DOC_LEVEL:    Level $DOC_LEVEL` and `Level $DOC_LEVEL Documentation (CORE + ADDENDUM v2.0)`
recommendation: Preserve current public names and reject any revised diff that adds `--preset`, `PRESET`, `KIND`, `CAPABILITIES`, or `MANIFEST_VERSION` to help, logs, or JSON.

title: Validator messages are mostly invariant-safe but remediation wording should be tightened
summary: check-files, check-sections, check-template-headers, check-section-counts, and validate summary output do not currently leak preset/capability/kind, but revised diffs should still make missing-file details and section-count remediation explicitly level-shaped.
evidence: check-files.sh passes with `All required files present for Level $level`
check-section-counts.sh warns with `Section counts below expectations for Level $declared_level`
check-section-counts.sh currently remediates with `Expand spec content or reduce declared level`
recommendation: Use `Level $level packet missing required file: decision-record.md` details and replace `reduce declared level` with `use the appropriate documentation level for this packet`.

title: Generated spec templates currently leak banned vocabulary
summary: The generated Level 3 and Level 3+ spec templates contain `[capability]` user-story placeholders, and the phase-parent template says `Sub-phase manifest`. These are AI-readable generated packet surfaces, so the workflow-invariance grep should fail today.
recommendation: Replace `[capability]` with `[needed behavior]` or `[user outcome]`; replace `Sub-phase manifest` with `Sub-phase map` or `Sub-phase list`.

title: description.json and graph-metadata.json should stay free of contract internals
summary: Current generated description.json and graph-metadata.json shapes do not include level contract internals, and they should not gain preset/capability/kind/manifestVersion fields because memory and graph parsers read them.
evidence: generate-description.js writes specFolder, description, keywords, lastUpdated, specId, folderSlug, parentChain, memorySequence, and memoryNameHistory
create_graph_metadata_file writes schema_version, packet_id, spec_folder, parent_id, children_ids, manual, and derived fields without contract internals
recommendation: Derive the level contract from SPECKIT_LEVEL at validation time. If persistence becomes necessary, store only a public derived.level_contract snapshot with level-shaped fields.

title: Internal manifest rows should be renamed to levelContracts
summary: The safest internal row name is `levelContracts[]`, not `presets[]`, because accidental exposure would still preserve level vocabulary and avoid teaching users a second taxonomy.
evidence: Iteration 11 left the question open because `presets[]` is leak-prone
The public resolver API is already named resolveLevelContract(level)
recommendation: Use `levelContracts[]` in the private schema and reserve arbitrary named presets for a future ADR if the public workflow contract is reopened.

title: manifestVersion should never enter AI-readable packet metadata
summary: manifestVersion belongs outside description.json, graph-metadata.json, frontmatter, and resume-read surfaces. Packet metadata should remain level-shaped or taxonomy-neutral.
evidence: description.json is consumed by memory folder discovery
graph-metadata.json is consumed by graph and memory parsers
Iteration 11 banned manifestVersion in AI-readable metadata unless hidden in a private non-prompted cache
recommendation: Pick option (a): never in packet metadata. Prefer runtime derivation; if needed, use a private implementation cache not read by resume or memory prompts.

summary: Ground-truth output audit completed across create.sh, validator rules, generated templates, description generation, and graph metadata creation.
before: Iteration 11 relied on revised diffs and known leakage risks from earlier proposals.
after: Iteration 12 maps the current emitted strings and exact JSON shapes against the invariant.

summary: Discovered current generated-template leaks missed by the proposed-diff review.
before: The invariant probe focused on CLI help, validator messages, JSON fields, and metadata keys.
after: Generated Level 3/3+ spec.md and phase-parent spec.md must also be cleaned because they contain banned words in AI-readable packet docs.

summary: Specified workflow-invariance CI test for user-facing surfaces.
before: No concrete test existed to fail builds when banned public vocabulary reappears.
after: Proposed scripts/tests/workflow-invariance.vitest.ts scans output-producing shell lines and generated templates for preset/capability/kind/manifest vocabulary.
